title: Guidelines
summary: Standards and best practices organised by key themes. Follow these guidelines to build services that meet Ministry of Justice and cross-government expectations.

sections.title: Project Inception
sections.description: Starting your project — understanding the problem, assessing feasibility, and planning your approach.

sections.title: Development & Iteration
sections.description: Building your service — coding standards, security practices, and agile delivery.

sections.title: Technology Choice
sections.description: Choosing the right tools — languages, frameworks, platforms, and open source.

sections.title: Standards & Best Practices
sections.description: Cross-cutting standards — accessibility, security, incident management, and operational excellence.

sections.title: Measuring Success
sections.description: Understanding impact — metrics, monitoring, service health, and user satisfaction.

items.title: Choosing a hosting platform
items.description: How to decide between Cloud Platform, Modernisation Platform, and other hosting options for your service.

items.title: Dependabot guidelines
items.description: Guidelines for using Dependabot to manage dependencies and security updates.

items.title: Preparing for service assessments
items.description: What to expect from GDS service assessments and how to prepare your team.

items.title: GOV.UK Service Manual
items.description: GDS guidance on researching, designing, building, and running government services.

items.title: Service Standard
items.description: The 14-point Service Standard that government services must meet.

items.title: API design standards
items.description: RESTful API design principles, versioning strategy, error handling, and pagination patterns.

items.title: Source control and branching
items.description: Git workflow standards, branch naming conventions, commit message formats, and code review expectations.

items.title: Security in development
items.description: Secure coding practices, dependency scanning, secret management, and threat modelling for development teams.

items.title: GDS API Technical & Data Standards
items.description: Cross-government standards for building and naming APIs, data formats, and integration patterns.

items.title: NCSC Secure Development & Deployment
items.description: National Cyber Security Centre collection on secure development lifecycle, deployment, and supply chain security.

items.title: Making technology choices
items.description: Framework for evaluating and choosing programming languages, frameworks, and tools for your service.

items.title: Coding in the open
items.description: When and how to make your code open source. Licensing, sensitive data, and the benefits of working in the open.

items.title: Technology Code of Practice
items.description: Cross-government guidance on how to design, build, and buy technology.

items.title: GOV.UK Design System
items.description: Styles, components, and patterns for designing government services.

items.title: Accessibility requirements
items.description: WCAG 2.2 AA compliance requirements, testing approach, and accessibility statements for government services.

items.title: AI Governance Framework
items.description: MoJ guidance on responsible AI use — risk assessment, transparency, accountability, and ethical considerations.

items.title: Incident management
items.description: How to handle production incidents — severity levels, communication, runbooks, and post-incident reviews.

items.title: NCSC Cloud Security Guidance
items.description: National Cyber Security Centre principles for securing cloud-hosted services.

items.title: Measuring service health
items.description: Key metrics, SLIs/SLOs, monitoring dashboards, and how to report on service performance.

items.title: Measuring service performance
items.description: GDS guidance on KPIs, cost per transaction, user satisfaction, and digital take-up.
